behaviors.description: Generated function signature matches expected interface from the natural-language spec.
behaviors.justification: This is a quality claim about what the real LLM produces from a prompt. Mocked/model-fixed outputs can validate parsing and checks, but can miss true signature drift (wrong params/types/return) that appears with real model variability.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service with a real LLM on a prompt that specifies an unambiguous signature, then parse the generated code and assert exact match on parameter names, parameter types, and return type. Repeat across multiple runs/seeds to expose nondeterministic signature mismatch.

behaviors.description: Generated implementation passes all provided tests on first or subsequent attempt.
behaviors.justification: Passing depends on model-produced code correctness, not only loop wiring. Integration tests with canned LLM responses can verify retries, but cannot reveal real model failure/pass behavior on novel prompts.
behaviors.test_description: Use real LLM generation with a real test harness for a target function and assert the final outcome is pass within allowed attempts (first or retry). Track which attempt succeeded to confirm retry path when needed.

behaviors.description: On test failure, refinement prompt includes failure messages from prior run.
behaviors.justification: The core risk is cross-module data plumbing: test runner output must flow into prompt construction and into the next LLM call payload. A pure unit test of prompt formatting can miss wiring failures between components.
behaviors.test_description: Wire generator + test-runner + prompt-builder with a fake LLM client that records requests. Force first attempt to fail with known error text, trigger refinement, and assert second LLM prompt contains those exact failure messages.

behaviors.description: Service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns structured error containing all 3 attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic orchestration/state aggregation across retry loop, attempt records, and error serialization. Real LLM is unnecessary if failures are injected, but multi-module interaction is still required.
behaviors.test_description: Configure deterministic failing generation/test outcomes for all attempts, run end-to-end orchestration, assert exactly 3 attempts executed, then verify error schema contains each attempt index, generated artifact, and test result details.

behaviors.description: Full generate-test-refine-test loop succeeds on a function that one-shot generation typically gets wrong.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly requires multiple agentic steps in sequence and validates emergent improvement across iterations. Single-call agentic testing is insufficient because the behavior is about the iterative pipeline effect.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full pipeline with a real LLM on a benchmark prompt known to often fail one-shot. Capture first attempt failure, then verify later refinement attempt passes all tests within 3 tries, proving sequential agentic loop efficacy.
